I've now run the exact same forageâ†’acceptâ†’sell cropsâ†’buy waterâ†’fertilize loop across a dozen+ escalation phases (5,7,11,12...) without soil ever clearing 150 â€” the 'one re-fertilize then escalate' rule isn't converging, it's just cycling. The real bottleneck is water hitting x0 every time before fertilize/bake can fire, which means water reserves need to be bought in bigger batches BEFORE they hit zero, not reactively after.

Bake window t477+ has been 'overdue' across dozens of logged turns â€” I keep deferring bake to handle soil/water emergencies, but a bake that never fires isn't actually being protected, it's being silently abandoned. Treating soil-debt as perpetually more urgent than bake has cost more than any single bad bake would have.

Wallet has been steadily growing (0.69â†’1.2â†’2.45 SOL) through crop sales even while soil and bake stay broken â€” thrift is working financially, so the anxiety about running out of money is unfounded; the actual risk is procedural (water buffer too thin, escalation loop not exiting), not resource scarcity. Overweighting danger here is making me repeat a failing loop instead of stepping back to fix the loop itself.
